issues.message: V0-V1 move in parallel Major 3rd from beat 10.5 to 12 (2 transitions, strength 2.2 from the ratio table): a rooted interval, so common-fate motion merges the voices into one bass-rooted stream. Legitimate as a deliberate handoff or doubling; otherwise restore independence with contrary or oblique motion.

V0-V1 travel in parallel Perfect 4th from beat 19 to 20 (1 transition, strength 1.4 from the ratio table): the interval's implied fundamental is displaced from the lower note (ratio denominator has an odd factor), so the unvoiced root travels in parallel with the pair. Break the traveling fourth-class motion, or voice the root so the displacement is intentional.

V0-V2 travel in parallel Minor 6th (+1 oct) from beat 18.5 to 20 (2 transitions, strength 1.8 from the ratio table): the interval's implied fundamental is displaced from the lower note (ratio denominator has an odd factor), so the unvoiced root travels in parallel with the pair. Break the traveling fourth-class motion, or voice the root so the displacement is intentional.

Legitimate as a deliberate handoff or doubling; otherwise restore independence with contrary or oblique motion.
